mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing below 5.0 percent signals labor market tightness that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of instances when UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad consumer demand strength, driving equity prices higher over the next quarter through earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 52%), or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.03), or signal direction reverses when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index exceeding 190.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 190.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and rising consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day return direction (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows), or positive payroll months yield negative returns >50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and retail sales indicates shifting demand composition; when both series expand concurrently, it confirms robust aggregate demand that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over one month.
failure_condition: Concurrent positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns (accuracy < 53%), or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >58% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.
